characters.name: Alex Smith
characters.description: Alex Smith, the third year medical student atCommunity General Hospitalwho appears in roughly 14 episodes of the series. He is also seen as a boxing student inNever Say Die(this episode stars the rest of Barry Van Dyke's children also.)

characters.name: Amanda Bentley
characters.description: Amanda Bentley(Victoria Rowell) is a medical examiner/pathologist atCommunity General Hospital. She appears in seasons 1-8 However, in the first three TV Movies (Diagnosis of Murder, The House on Sycamore Street and A Twist of the Knife), Amanda is played by Cynthia Gibb. Victoria Rowell appeared in all the episodes of the series, with the exception of 11 (both sporadically and during the last season) on (CBS). Dr. Amanda Bentley isDr. Mark Sloan'sfaithful and beautiful colleague and medical partner, specializing in pathology. It is revealed in the series that the character of Amanda is an orphan (just as Victoria Rowell is in real life), taken to an orphanage when she was younger and soon adopted. Her adopted father was an owner of a yacht, and his sailors were always good friends with the family. When she was six, Amanda took up ballet. She stopped dancing for medical school, but returns to it at the end of season eight. She once fainted during medical school at the sight of her first corpse. She is constantly kidded about it in the first two seasons. It was revealed in season 1 that she was considering changing her specialty. At one point during the show Amanda's character is married once to Mr. Colin Livingston, a military man. In later episodes it is made clear that she is separated from Colin, however whether this is the result of his death in a plane crash or a divorce varies from one episode to the next.

characters.name: Caitlin Sweeney
characters.description:
Caitlin Sweeneyis the daughter ofLouis Sweeney, the Sunnyview bomber, and sister toCarter Sweeney.
She is also the one responsible for bombing Community General Hospital in Resurrection Pt 1.

characters.name: Carter Sweeney
characters.description: Carter Sweeneyis the son of Sunnyview bomber,Louis Sweeneyand brother ofCaitlin Sweeney. He has his own radio show which he used to try to discreditMarkwith.

characters.name: Delores Mitchell
characters.description: NurseDelores Mitchell(Delores Hall) wasDr. Sloan'slively secretary who appeared in seasons 1–2

characters.name: Delores Mitchell
characters.description: NurseDelores Mitchell(Delores Hall) wasDr. Sloan'slively secretary who appeared in seasons 1–2

characters.name: Harry Trumble
characters.description: Harry Trumbleis a retired police Officer who was with theLos Angeles Police Departmentfor forty years. He spent his last few years focused on the case of the "'The Clown Killer". He appears in "Voices Carry" where he is so dismayed that he never managed to catch the clown killer that he fakes evidence so that the case stays open. This unfortunately gives the real clown killer an opportunity to start killing again.

characters.name: Jack Stewart
characters.description: Jack Stewartis a doctor atCommunity General Hospitalwho appeared in the first two seasons of the show. He was Steve's best friend, whom he often helped in his cases. He left to open his own practice in Colorado.

characters.name: Jack Stewart
characters.description: Jack Stewartis a doctor atCommunity General Hospitalwho appeared in the first two seasons of the show. He was Steve's best friend, whom he often helped in his cases. He left to open his own practice in Colorado.

characters.name: Jesse Travis
characters.description: Dr.Jesse Travis(Charlie Schlatter)  as regular character in seasons 3-8. According to it background revealed in the show, he was born in Minnesota. His mother's name is never mentioned, but she was portrayed by Kathleen Noone. She is a podiatrist and owns a family practice in Minnesota. She is always trying to convince Jesse to move back. His father is Dane Travis (Robert Culp), who is a retired CIA spy and tennis professional. His parents are divorced. Charlie Schlatter almost never missed a single episode from the moment he joined the cast in 1995, and was one of the breakout characters. He was absent for only five shows (1 near the end of the fourth season and 4 in the eighth and final season). When Jesse was little, his father gave him his prized Bourgandi violin. It is appraised at $15,000. Jesse also was given a valuable wristwatch from his father and can be seen wearing it in every episode.After medical school, Jesse became a resident at Community General, where his teacher, Dr. Mark Sloan would inspire him to be a doctor. (Charlie Schlatter was introduced in Season Three of Diagnosis: Murder to replace Scott Baio, who'd left the show.) He loves salt and puts it on everything. He often helps Mark and Steve with investigations.At about the same time, Jesse and Steve go into business together. They co-own a barbecue restaurant calledBBQ Bob's.

characters.name: Louis Sweeney
characters.description: Louis Sweeneywas the head of the Sweeney family who father a son,Carter Sweeney, and a daughter,Caitlin Sweeney. He is a convicted serial bomber known as the Sunnyview bomber who terrorized the LA area. TheLAPDstarted trying to build a case against Louis and with the help ofMark Sloanhe was convicted of the bombings. He was executed for his crimes in "Obsession" which is when hissonanddaughterrestarted the bombing campaign.

characters.name: Mark Sloan
characters.description: Dr. Mark Sloan, played by Dick Van Dyke, has been in medicine for over 40 years and is chief of internal medicine atCommunity General Hospitaland a consultant toLAPD. Becoming a doctor made him the black sheep of the family as he came from "a long line of cops" with his father was a police detective. He often refers to himself as an amateur sleuth who loves solving mysteries. Mark has an older sister, Dora, who owns a travel agency. He also has a younger brother, Stacey, who owns a malt shop in Arkansas and has a tendency to sleepwalk. Mark is a widower, since his wife Katherine died of cancer a little over 10 years ago. With Katherine, he had two children. His sonSteve(with whom Mark shares hisDenver Town Houselater theMalibu Beach House) is lieutenant and police detective for the LAPD. Mark also has an estranged daughter, Carol Sloan Hilton (Stacey Van Dyke), a nurse, who lives in Northern California. Mark has an older sister, Dora, who owns a travel agency. He also has a younger brother, Stacey, who owns a malt shop in Arkansas and has a tendency to sleepwalk. Mark is a widower, since his wife Katherine died of cancer a little over 10 years ago. With Katherine, he had two children. His sonSteve(with whom Mark shares hisDenver Town Houselater theMalibu Beach House) is lieutenant and police detective for the LAPD. Mark also has an estranged daughter, Carol Sloan Hilton (Stacey Van Dyke), a nurse, who lives in Northern California. His first home was a small house at Colorado Drive, Los Angeles. Sometime later, he buys a beach house is located at Beach Drive, Malibu.To pay for medical school, Mark served in the Army during the Korean War. Mark likes jazz and Glenn Miller, enjoys dancing (soft shoe and tap). He can play a number of instruments including the clarinet and keyboards. He is good at chess and can often be found rollerblading when his car breaks down. His party piece is magic tricks.

characters.name: Mary Ann Eagin
characters.description: The villainous Mary Ann Eagin, played by Marina Sirtis Mary Ann Eaginis the villainess from "Murder at the Finish Line" She is the owner of Eagin Racing, but she was revealed to be having financial problems. She used the money raised for her team to cover her losses, but she oversold her shares to various investors. Once Matt Thomas, one of her noted racers, started winning numerous races, Mary Ann turned heel and set out to stop his winning streak, mainly to keep the investors from wanting a return on her money. She poisoned Matt hours before his race, and to make sure he would be killed, she sabotaged the engine in his car, leading to his crash.

characters.name: Norman Briggs
characters.description: Norman Briggs, played by Michael Tucci, is the administrator atCommunity General Hospitaland helped bring it back from the brink of closure. He became a close friend ofMark, even though he wasn't always happy about some of the things Mark did. Norman has a tendency to get overexcited and finds it hard to relax, which leads to a very stressful life. He tries to help and do his best for others, but he just can’t calm down and often ends up hurting himself in one way or another. Norman Briggs attended Yale University, graduating in 1978 where he was voted "Most Popular". After college, he served in the Peace Corps in Nigeria before pursuing a career in hospital administration (seasons 1–4). He joined Community General Hospital as Head of Administration two years before Dr. Jack Stewart and Dr. Amanda Bentley arrived. During his tenure, he became a close friend ofDr. Mark Sloan, though he often expressed frustration that Sloan's consulting for the LAPD took attention away from patient care. To placate Briggs, Sloan sometimes downplayed or concealed his involvement in police cases. Norman Briggs attended Yale University, graduating in 1978 where he was voted "Most Popular". After college, he served in the Peace Corps in Nigeria before pursuing a career in hospital administration (seasons 1–4). He joined Community General Hospital as Head of Administration two years before Dr. Jack Stewart and Dr. Amanda Bentley arrived. During his tenure, he became a close friend ofDr. Mark Sloan, though he often expressed frustration that Sloan's consulting for the LAPD took attention away from patient care. To placate Briggs, Sloan sometimes downplayed or concealed his involvement in police cases. Throughout the series, Briggs frequently mentioned his family—particularly his mother, Minnie Briggs, and his sister, Marie Briggs—revealing his underlying concern for their opinions. While not universally popular among the hospital staff, Briggs's efforts to balance the budget and secure Community General's finances were instrumental in keeping the hospital operational and prosperous. After departing Community General at the end of season 4, he volunteered at the Malone Foundation, where he took pleasure in giving to charity rather than "pinching pennies".

characters.name: Steve Sloan
characters.description: Steve Sloanis the son ofDr. Mark Sloanand Mark's wife, Katherine. Katherine died of cancer when Steve was younger—throughout the series, Mark refers to her death occurring "ten years ago." Steve has one sibling, an estranged sister namedCarol. (Carol is only mentioned in a few episodes.) Right out of college, he joined the army and spent 18 months on one tour to Vietnam during the War. He took a second tour towards the end of the war. Afterwards, Steve joined the LAPD and, after season two, was promoted from homicide detective to lieutenant. Following this appointment, Steve was subsequently promoted to his own one man army, in his genuine pursuit of justice His apartment burns down, he moves to a separate apartment in his father'sMalibu beach house. He often tries to move away, once even buying an electric house. After the house almost killsMark, Steve begins renting it. Steve was once shot in the chest and was legally dead for three minutes until doctors were able to resuscitate him. This makes him a target in "Murder On The Hour"

characters.name: Susan Hillard
characters.description: Susan Hillard(Kim Little) is a nurse working atCommunity General Hospitalwho starts datingJesse Travisfor a number of episodes. A running joke in the series is that every time Susan and Jesse try to plan a romantic trip together something big happends to stop them.

characters.name: Susan Hillard
characters.description: Susan Hillard(Kim Little) is a nurse working atCommunity General Hospitalwho starts datingJesse Travisfor a number of episodes. A running joke in the series is that every time Susan and Jesse try to plan a romantic trip together something big happends to stop them.

characters.name: Tanis Archer
characters.description: Tanis Archer is Steve's partner on the police force in seasons 5 and 6. She first appears in Retribution part 1 and follows the evidence that framed Mark. She and Chief Masters arrest him and Mark receives the death sentence. In Retribution part 2 she is seen outside the prison where Mark is and tells Steve that he should be mad at her since she arrested his father. He isn't mad at her and the two of them work together to prove that Mark is innocent. She also admits to the Chief that she was wrong in arresting Mark.

characters.name: The Clown Killer
characters.description: The Clown Killeris an American serial killer who appeared within "Voices Carry". The killer is a famous serial killer who years before the episode was killing people in the LA area and painting clown faces on his victims. LAPDpolice officerHarry Trumblewas assigned to lead the task force to catch the killer but the original investigation never did so and it was wound down as the killings stopped. MarkandStevetheorize that the killings didn't stop but the killer moved to different counties all over the world where he carried on killing. In the episode Harry fakes evidence to make it look like the killer is back which the killer takes as an offer to start killing again.
